title: Rich Fluffy Algerian Emmentaler Cheese Frittata
description: A fresh and fluffy Algerian dish perfect for brunch, featuring Emmentaler cheese and made in an iron teppan, this recipe is shojin-friendly and kneaded to perfection.
instructions: Preheat the iron teppan over medium heat and add the butter to melt.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, salt, and black pepper.
Add the grated Emmentaler cheese to the egg mixture and stir to combine.
Pour the egg mixture over the onion and garlic in the teppan.
Cook the mixture for about 5 minutes, until the edges start to set.
Use a spatula to gently lift and fold the edges of the frittata towards the center.
Continue cooking and folding the frittata for an additional 5 minutes, until the eggs are almost set.
Remove the teppan from the heat and sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top of the frittata.
Let the frittata cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
